2025-04-04.thesis: China's Announcement 18 curbs medium/heavy rare-earth exports
2025-04-04.exit_case: none — Announcement 18 curbs just took effect this week; export control regime freshly imposed, not resolved
2025-04-04.assessment: China's Ministry of Commerce Announcement 18 imposes export controls on seven medium/heavy rare earths (samarium, gadolinium, terbium, etc.) via licensing requirements, directly threatening US/allied defense and magnet supply chains. MP Materials is the lone scaled US producer/processor and the cleanest pure-play; Lynas (LYC) is the leading ex-China separator with heavy rare earth capability in Malaysia; TMRC and UUUU are smaller US-based rare earth/critical mineral developers that benefit from the same reshoring-of-supply-chain narrative. This is a structural, open-ended catalyst (China's rare-earth leverage as a standing curb regime) likely to generate ongoing milestones — license backlogs, further curb expansions, US stockpiling/funding responses, allied supply deals. Early days; catalyst is specific, datable, and still under-reported relative to its magnitude.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Hold — this is entry week, curbs are freshly announced and China's licensing regime for medium/heavy rare earths is only beginning; watch for implementation details, export license backlogs, and Western supply-chain responses (DoD funding, price floors, offtake deals) as concrete next milestones.

2025-12-19.assessment: Thirty-sixth week since entry. This week's news flow (Sensex/TSX market rallies on US CPI data, renewable/nuclear stock screeners, India AI-stock bubble fears, Canadian equities) has zero bearing on Announcement 18 or the rare-earth curb regime specifically. The driving condition - China's licensing requirement on samarium, gadolinium, terbium, and other medium/heavy rare earths - remains standing, unreversed, unresolved; no confirmed rollback has ever been reported, even after the Oct 30 Trump-Xi summit and Nov 1 tariff deadline both passed without rare-earth-specific resolution. Eight consecutive weeks of silence on the specific trend continue to compound the fade per silence decay, now deeply pinned at the cull floor. This is not arc completion (no confirmed counter-event/curb lifting reported; potential government equity stakes in miners remain an unconfirmed but not-yet-dead concrete pending item) and not mainstream saturation of rare-earth-curb mechanics specifically - coverage of this ticker-trend has simply gone quiet for two months now. Basket unchanged - MP remains cleanest US pure-play with direct DoD equity backing, LYC the leading ex-China separator, TMRC/UUUU smaller US developers riding the same reshoring narrative; no individual vehicle's thesis has broken, but conviction stays at floor pending fresh catalyst-specific coverage.
2025-12-19.exit_advice: Hold basket but at floor conviction; structural driver still intact (no curb lifting, no counter-event), just prolonged silence on ticker-specific coverage - eighth consecutive quiet week. Re-escalation (new curbs, tariff threats, potential govt equity stakes) two months ago proved the driver still throws off fresh milestones when it does; absent that, this is a pure silence-decay hold, not a fundamental break. Would exit only on confirmed formal rollback/lifting of the licensing requirements.
